hook_line: Muddy paws do not have to hit your clean floor.
business_purpose: Position the product as a quick after-walk cleanup tool
first_principles_reason: The scene is instantly recognizable and the pain is visible before explanation; the clean home relief trigger connects the product to a fast prevention job; that direct problem-to-solution path supports stronger early retention and later clicks

hook_line: Bath time should not turn into a wet floor cleanup.
business_purpose: Connect the product to reducing home mess
first_principles_reason: The scene names a common messy moment and the pain is cleanup labor; the less cleanup emotion makes the benefit practical; showing a simpler rinse path gives viewers a reason to keep watching through the demo

hook_line: Skip the long bath and start with a quick rinse.
business_purpose: Frame the product as a lower-friction grooming helper
first_principles_reason: The scene offers a faster alternative to a disliked task and the pain is bath friction; the faster routine emotion reduces resistance; a lower-effort use case can make the product feel immediately clickable

hook_line: If your dog hates baths try a faster rinse first.
first_principles_reason: The scene focuses on a dog behavior owners recognize and the pain is emotional struggle; empathy makes viewers feel understood; naming the struggle can invite comments and make the product demo more relevant

hook_line: Rainy walk cleanup can happen before they run inside.
first_principles_reason: The scene ties to a predictable weather routine and the pain is indoor mess; control before mess creates urgency; preventing damage before it happens is a simple commercial reason to watch and consider the tool
